ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for almond jelly with longan; closest match is jelly dessert food product (FOODON_03315763). Almond jelly is an agar-based set dessert distinct from gelatin-based Western jellies.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP used as closest defensible match for agar-based jelly dessert. No specific code exists for almond jelly with longan in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for almond jelly with longan; this is a food product not typically coded in clinical terminology systems.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of almond jelly with longan is approximately 200 g, served as a chilled dessert bowl containing almond jelly cubes and longan fruit with syrup.

nutrient_source.title: Almond Jelly with Longan — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Almond Jelly with Longan (cr_id F02030003). All per-100g values are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous agar jelly dessert entry as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — agar jelly / sugar-sweetened jelly dessert analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for almond jelly with longan in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is considered 'low' according to the University of Sydney GI Database classification (GI <= 55 is low). This value is estimated by analogy to sugar-sweetened agar-based jelly desserts, which have reported GI values in the range 50–60 in peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2021, Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per 200g serving calculated as (55 × 16.4 × 2) / 100 = 18.0g. Input record GI confidence was 0.375, reflecting the analogous nature of this estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (15.5g/100g) with zero dietary fibre drives insulin-mediated sodium retention despite low sodium (14mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit or avoid this dessert because its high free-sugar load (15.5g/100g, 31g per 200g serving) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and vascular inflammation, offsetting the benefit of its low sodium content; if consumed, choose a version made with fresh longan rather than canned longan in syrup to reduce sugar intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 15.5g/100g with dietary fibre 0.0g/100g — rapid postprandial glucose spike expected with no fibre modulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this dessert; a 200g serving delivers approximately 31g of free sugar with zero dietary fibre, producing a rapid and unmodulated postprandial glucose rise that is incompatible with glycaemic management targets, and the low GI of 55 combined with high glycaemic load (GL ~18 per serving) further elevates risk.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar content (15.5g/100g) with negligible fibre promotes dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation; low saturated fat and sodium are partial mitigating factors
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should consume this dessert only occasionally and in small portions; while saturated fat and sodium are low, the high free-sugar content (15.5g/100g) is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and endothelial dysfunction per AHA dietary guidelines, and a 200g serving exceeds the WHO daily free-sugar limit of 25g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Almond jelly with longan is a popular chilled Chinese dessert widely served in Singapore, Hong Kong, and across Southeast Asia. It consists of smooth, silky cubes of almond-flavoured agar-agar jelly — made by setting a sweetened mixture of agar powder, almond milk or almond essence, and sugar — served in a bowl with longan fruit, typically canned longan in light syrup. The jelly has a delicate, mildly sweet almond flavour and a firm yet tender texture that melts on the palate. The longan contributes a floral sweetness and soft, translucent flesh. Per 100g, the dish provides 75 kcal, 1.2g protein, 0.6g fat, 16.4g carbohydrate (of which 15.5g is sugar), 0.0g dietary fibre, and 14mg sodium. Calcium is notably present at 42mg/100g from the almond base. The dessert is low in sodium and calories but high in free sugar with zero dietary fibre, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The agar-agar base is plant-derived, making this dessert vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is commonly served at Chinese restaurants, hawker centres, and festive occasions in Singapore. Clinically, the combination of high free sugar and zero fibre produces rapid postprandial glucose absorption, and a standard 200g serving delivers approximately 31g of free sugar, exceeding the WHO daily free-sugar recommendation of 25g.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F02030003) with confidence 0.95. This is an exact match to the canonical HPB record for this dish. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence) as no direct GI measurement exists for this specific preparation.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — this agar-based dessert contains no meaningful dietary fibre; iron_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific almond jelly with longan preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. saturated_fat_g (0.1g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous agar jelly dessert entry as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item. 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened agar-based jelly desserts per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021); no direct GI measurement exists for almond jelly with longan. 5. cholesterol_mg set to 0.0 — this is a plant-based dessert containing no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero by formulation. 6. Canned longan in syrup significantly elevates sugar content versus fresh longan; consumers are advised to request fresh longan where available.
